prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation for pyridine (11 atoms: N, C×5, H×5) using DFT at tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI, PySCF RKS) — gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, used as-is (no re-optimization; this is a single-point on the supplied geometry). SCF converged in 10 cycles. No warnings reported. Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true, all checks passed.

Key results:
  HOMO (index 20): −7.2525992 eV
  LUMO (index 21): −1.1127860 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap:   6.1398131 eV  ← headline value

Koopmans-theorem derived quantities:
  Vertical IP  = 7.2525992 eV
  Vertical EA  = 1.1127860 eV
  Electronegativity χ = 4.1826926 eV
  Chemical hardness η = 3.0699066 eV
  Chemical softness σ = 0.32574281 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity ω  = 2.8494218 eV

The gap is a Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an optical excitation energy. Density fitting (RI) to two-electron integrals is on by default and is part of the honest method description. No warnings were reported by the engine (warnings array is empty).

Live log: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260707-215834_pyridine_frontier_orbitals/frontier_20260707-215843.out

CLI: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz
